arts.ttl: Chelsea Transfer News: Enzo Maresca begs Blues to change signing policy
arts.sum: Speaking to the media ahead of Chelsea's game against West Ham, Maresca admitted that the Blues are still some way off the likes of Man City and Arsenal due to the lack of stability in recent seasons. “I think we are a very good squad but there are clubs like Manchester City and Arsenal that are ahead of us, no doubt.

arts.ttl: Chelsea team news: Enzo Maresca makes three changes for West Ham United fixture
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca makes three changes for Saturday's Premier League fixture versus West Ham United at the London Stadium.

arts.ttl: Chelsea hope to sign star amid Barcelona’s financial issues
arts.sum: Chelsea are hoping to tempt Barcelona into the sale of Jules Kounde as the Blues look to capitalise on the economic issues in Catalonia. Barcelona’s challenging financial situation means sales could be required to fund further transfer business, with the Spanish side having had to offload players to register new recruit Dani Olmo this summer. […] The post Chelsea hope to sign star amid Barcelona’s financial issues first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: West Ham vs Chelsea: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: A London derby kicks off Gameweek 5 of the 2024/25 Premier League season when Chelsea make the short journey across the capital to take on West Ham United on Saturday lunchtime. Julen Lopetegui's Hammers have had some tough games to start the term but salvaged a late draw at Fulham last time out, while the Blues registered their second league win under Enzo Maresca with a slender 1-0 success at Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Paquetá and Summerville in team for Chelsea derby
arts.sum: West Ham United: Areola, Wan-Bissaka, Mavropanos, Kilman, Emerson, Rodríguez, Álvarez, Paquetá, Kudus, Summerville, Bowen © Alongside Antonio and Souček on the substitutes’ bench will be goalkeeper Łukasz Fabiański (GK), defenders Vladimír Coufal, Aaron Cresswell and Jean-Clair Todibo, midfielders Carlos Soler and Andy Irving and forward Danny Ings.
